batchReplay.finiteScopeConclusion: All five remaining observed top-only groups were replayed from raw tuple-row CRT data. Two are strict target-first finite replay certificates; three preserve the target square-witness residue but expose earlier or co-tied tracked activations, so they become deterministic profile-boundary successors instead of universal coverage.

groupClassifications.proofBoundary: Finite observed top-only group replay only; target-first replay is certified but this is not a universal square-witness domain theorem.

Finite observed top-only group replay reaches and preserves the target square-witness residue, but earlier or co-tied tracked activations block a target-only coverage theorem without a profile handoff.

nonFirstTargetSuccessorBoundary.requiredResolution: For each non-first-target boundary, prove the earlier/co-tied tracked activation is already covered by the contrast/handoff theorem surface, or emit a counterfamily/profile packet before any 40501+ rollout.
nonFirstTargetSuccessorBoundary.deterministicBoundary: The only replay failure for these groups is firstTrackedMatchIsTarget; target square obstruction and CRT preservation are certified at finite observed scope.

nextTheoremMove: Resolve the three non-first-target top-only profile boundaries by proving their earlier/co-tied tracked activations are contrast/handoff-covered, or emit the next deterministic counterfamily packet before any 40501+ rollout.
proofBoundary: This packet batch-classifies the remaining observed top-only mod-50 witness groups at finite replay scope. It does not prove the universal square-witness domain cover, does not cover contrast-only groups, does not justify a 40501+ rollout, and does not decide Problem 848.
